title: Run Support Ops
subtitle: A small starting shape for support work where request flow and operator tooling matter first.
scope: One support or operations domain where requests enter through a mediated path and operators need a clear tool layer.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace placeholder request paths, workflow references, and tooling paths with real support sources.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when support work is still simple and you mainly need to show how requests flow and which tools operators depend on.

objective: Make the request path and operator tool layer legible enough that someone can quickly understand how the support domain currently runs.
successCondition: A reader can see how a request enters the system, who or what fulfills it, and which tools the operators trust.

callouts.title: How to use this starter
items: Focus first on the main request flow rather than trying to catalogue every support lane.
Add operating surfaces and enablers once the support model starts branching.

callout.title: Starter intent
items: Keep the top of the document about operational reality, not background context.
The detailed request flow belongs in the operation section below.

data.name: Incoming request intake
data.currentSupport: Operators can accept structured requests and route them into a known fulfillment path.
data.nextStep: Add stronger validation for underspecified requests before scaling the flow further.
notes: Write the main request path first. You can branch into more surfaces later.

data.name: Support router
data.purpose: Normalize incoming support requests and route them into the right operator lane.
data.whenToUse: Use when manual triage starts becoming the main source of delay.
caveats: Prototype routing logic should not be treated as authoritative until its failure modes are understood.
